title: dianthus algetanus subsp. turolensis
intro: Dianthus algetanus subsp. turolensis is a plant species belonging to the carnation family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and sweet fragrance. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes. The health benefits of Dianthus algetanus subsp. turolensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be employed to leverage its medicinal properties.
